provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Labour Institutions Act; “ Acts ” means the Labour Institutions Act , and the Employment and Labour Relations Act ; [Cap. 300; Cap. 366] “ Commission ” means the Commission for Mediation and Arbitration established under section 12 of the Act ; “ Committee ” means the Essential Services Committee established under section 29 of the Act ; “ Council ” means the Labour, Economic and Social Council established under section 3 of the Act ; “ Director ” means the Director of the Commission appointed under section 18 of the Act and includes a staff member of the Commission appointed to act on his behalf in matters relating to essential services ; “ dispute ” means a dispute of interest and any other dispute on essential services ; “ document ” includes any publication and any matter written, expressed or described upon any substance by means of letters, figures, or marks, or by more than one of those means which in intended to be used for the purpose of recording that matter, document or record in electronic form; “ electronic ” means anything relating to technology having electrical, digital, magnetic, wireless, optical, electromagnetic, or similar capabilities; “ essential services ” means any of the services provided for in section 77(2) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act and any other service as may be designated as such by the Committee ; [Cap. 366] “ Labour Court ” means the Labour Division of the High Court established under section 50 of the Act ; “ meeting ” means any meeting convened by the Committee for the conduct of its meeting under section 33(1) of the Act ; “ part ” means a party to any proceedings under these Regulations; “ record ” means a record in a written or electronic form and includes tape recording; “ representative ” means any person authorized to represent a party under these Regulations; “ Secretariat ” means the Secretariat referred to under section 33(2) of the Act ’. Part II – Designation of essential service

provisions.title: Designa­tion of essential service
provisions.content: The Committee may, in the exercise of the powers conferred on it by section 77(3) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , designate a service as an essential service if the interruption of that service endangers the personal safety or health of the population or any part of it. [Cap. 366]

provisions.title: Notice for represen­tation
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of section 77(4) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , the Committee shall issue a notice in at least two widely circulated newspapers of its intention to designate such service as an essential service inviting any person to make written representations within fourteen days of the giving notice in the newspapers. [Cap. 366] (2) The notice under sub-regulation (1) shall be made in ESC. Form No. 1 prescribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Conduct of public hearing
provisions.content: (1) In addition to the written representations received by the Committee under regulation 4 , the Committee shall hold public hearing or hearings in the area or areas in which the service being investigated is situated and invite interested parties in that area or areas to attend the hearing or hearings and make oral representations or submissions which shall be recorded by the Committee. (2) The Committee shall before conducting the public hearing or in any area publish a notice, in, at least two widely circulating newspapers, stating the time, date, day and place at which the public hearing or hearings shall be conducted. (3) The notice under sub-regulation (2) shall be issued at least seven days before the hearing.

provisions.title: Factual report
provisions.content: (1) The Committee may request the Secretariat to prepare a factual report concerning any service that the Committee is investigating. (2) The Committee shall notify any person who made written representations under regulation 4 of the existence of a factual report relevant to the investigation and invite that person to submit written representation on the report to the Committee within fourteen days of the notification. (3) The notification under sub-regulation (2) shall be in ESC Form No. 2 prescribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (4) Any person may inspect any written representations made pursuant to the provisions of regulation 4 and any factual report relevant to the investigation at the Commission’s Head Office or any other place as the Commission may decide.

provisions.title: Variation and cancella­tion of a service
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of section 77(6) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , Committee may vary or cancel the designation of a service as an essential service by following the procedure set out in the provisions of regulations 4 , 5 , and 6 . (2) The Committee shall make a decision on the designation, variation or cancellation of an essential service within fourteen days after the expiry of the seven days’ notice referred to in regulation 5(2) or after the receipt of the written representations made under regulation 6(2) . (3) The Committee shall within thirty days of its decision to designate, cancel or vary a service as an essential service notify the parties and publish a notice to that effect in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Right of appeal
provisions.content: Any person aggrieved by the decision of the Committee may within thirty days of the decision, appeal to the Labour Court . Part III – Serving of documents and determination of disputes

provisions.title: Referral of disputes to the Com­mittee
provisions.content: (1) A referral of a dispute to the Committee under section 77(7) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , shall be made in ESC Form No. 3 of the First Schedule to these Regulations. (2) The other parties to the dispute or parties interested in the dispute may submit written representations within fourteen days of receipt of the referral form by the Committee unless the referral is an urgent referral in which case the Committee shall notify the parties of the period in which they must submit written representations. (3) The Committee may require the parties to submit any further information relevant to the dispute in writing. (4) The Committee may give directions to the parties for the service of any documents. [Cap. 366]

provisions.title: Proof of service
provisions.content: (1) Any party to a dispute as to whether or not a service is an essential service or whether an employer or employee is engaged in an essential service shall refer the dispute to the Committee for determination. (2) Whenever a party is required to satisfy the Committee that a copy of a referral, objection or other document has been served on another party, the party so required must provide the Committee with: (a) a copy of the proof of mailing the referral, objection or other document by registered post to the other party; (b) a copy of the telegram, telex or telefax communicating the referral, objection or other document to the other party; (c) a copy of a receipt signed by the other party or on that party’s behalf if the copy of the referral, objection or other document was delivered by hand; or (d) a statement confirming service signed by the person who delivered a copy of the referral, objection or other document to the other party.

provisions.title: Applica­tion by employer for designa­tion of minimum services
provisions.content: (1) An application to the Committee by an employer for designation of a minimum service in order to prevent damage to property, machinery or plant during a strike or lawful lockout under section 79(3) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , shall be made in Form ESC No. 4 prescribed the First Schedule to these Regulations. (2) An application made by an employer under sub-regulation (1) shall be dealt with by the Committee in accordance with the procedure set out in regulation 12 .

provisions.title: Conduct of hearings of dispute or applica­tion
provisions.content: (1) Within fourteen days after the receipt of the referral or application contemplated in terms of sections 9(7) and 79(3) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , and regulation 9(2) respectively, the Committee shall notify the parties in writing of the place and time of the hearing using ESC Form No. 5 prescribed in to the First Schedule to these Regulations. (2) In determining the place and time for hearing the committee shall take due regard to urgency of the matter. (3) The Committee may decide whether the dispute is capable of determination on the written representations before it or that it requires to hear oral evidence from the parties. (4) If the Committee decides that the matter is capable of determination on the written representations before it, the Committee may hear argument from the parties on their representations and proceed to make its determination. (5) If the Committee decides that it requires to hear oral evidence— (a) if the Committee shall decide the issues on which it requires to hear oral evidence; and (b) notify the parties of its decision in good time but not later than thirty days after the decision thereof. (6) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulation (3) ; the Committee, during a hearing, may direct that oral evidence be heard in which case the provisions of sub-regulation (4) will apply. (7) If the Committee decides that the matter is urgent, the Committee may make an interim order determining the dispute pending a final determination. (8) Where the Committee issues the intern orders the Committee shall specify a date in the order, which date may not be later than the date on which the interim order expires, for a hearing to determine the dispute . (9) The Committee shall conduct the hearing in a manner that the Committee considers appropriate in order to make a determination with the minimum of legal formalities and within seven days after closure of the hearing. (10) The Committee shall provide the parties with a brief summary of the reasons for its decision.

provisions.title: Right of appeal
provisions.content: Any party aggrieved by the decision of the Committee may appeal to the Labour Court .

provisions.title: Oath
provisions.content: A member of the Committee shall, before entering upon the duties of office, take and subscribed to an oath for the due performance of the functions of office as set out in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. Part IV – General provisions

provisions.title: Issuance and serving of summons
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of section 32 of the Act , the Committee shall have power to issue and serve summons against any person. (2) A summons issued under sub-regulation (1) shall be as in the ESC Form No. 6 prescribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (3) A summons issued for any purpose under sub-regulation (1) shall be signed by the Director, or any member of the Secretariat and the summons— (a) specifically require the person named in it to appear before the Committee ; (b) sufficiently identify the book, document or object to be produced; and (c) state the date, day, time and place at which the person is to appear. (4) The law relating to privilege, as it applies to a witness summoned to give evidence or to produce any book, document or object before a court of law, shall apply equally to the questioning of any person or the production or seizure of any book, document or object in terms of this regulation. (5) Where any person, expert or witness is summoned or called by the Committee under this regulation the costs for that person, expert or witness shall be borne out by the Director .

provisions.title: Address for service on Committee
provisions.content: Whenever a person is required to serve any document on the Committee , such service shall be on the Commission which is the Secretariat of the Committee .

provisions.title: Power and procedure for conduct­ing investiga­tions
provisions.content: (1) The Committee may, where it considers appropriate, conduct an investigation. (2) In conducting an investigation, the Committee may— (a) at any reasonable time, but after obtaining the necessary written authorization— (i) enter and investigate the premises on or in which any book, document or object relevant to an investigation or a hearing is to be found or is suspected on reasonable grounds of being found there; (ii) examine, demand the production of, and seize any book, document or object that is on or in those premises and that is relevant to an investigation or a hearing; (iii) take a statement in respect of any matter relevant to an investigation or a hearing from any person on the premises who is willing to make a statement; (iv) retain for not mote than thirty days, any of the books, documents or objects that have been produced to, or seized by, the Committee ; (b) call, and if necessary, any expert to appear before the Committee to give evidence relevant to an investigation or a hearing; (c) call any person present at an investigation or hearing who was or could have been summoned for any purpose set out in these Regulations, to be questioned about any matter relevant to an investigation or a hearing. (3) The written authorization referred to in sub-regulation (1) may be given by a judge of the Labour Court only if the judge is satisfied that the exercise of the powers referred to in the said sub-regulation, is necessary for the performance of the Committee’s functions. (4) The application for grant or refusal of the authorization shall be made in the ESC Form No. 7 prescribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (5) The owner or occupier of any premises that the Committee is authorized to enter and investigate, and every person employed by that owner or occupier, shall provide any facilities that the Committee requires to enter those premises and to carry out the investigation or seizure. (6) The Committee shall issue a receipt in the name of the Commission for any book, document or object seized under sub-regulation (2) .

provisions.title: Contempt against the Com­mittee
provisions.content: (1) A person commits contempt against the Committee if— (a) after having been summoned to appear before the Committee , the person, without good cause, does not attend at the time and place started in the summons; (b) after having appeared in response to a summons, that person fails to remain in attendance until excused by the Committee ; (c) by refusing to take the oath or to make an affirmation as a witness when the Committee so requires; (d) he refuses to answer any question fully and to the best of that person’s knowledge and belief subject to regulation 15(4) ; (e) the person, without good cause, fails to produce any book, document or object specified in a summons to the Committee ; (f) he willfully hinders the Committee in performing any function conferred on it by or under the Acts ; (g) he insults, disparages or belittles the Committee , or prejudices or improperly influences the proceedings or improperly anticipates the Committee ’s decision or determination; (h) he willfully interrupts the proceedings of the Committee or misbehaving in any other manner during those proceedings; and (i) he does anything else in relation to the Committee which, if done in relation to a court of law, would have been contempt of court. (2) Any person who commits contempt against the Committee shall be liable to a penalty as provided for under section 114 of the Penal Code . [Cap. 16]

provisions.title: Meetings and dis­charge of functions of the Com­mittee
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the tripartite principle, the quorum for any meeting of the Committee shall be not less than three members. (2) If three or more members of the Committee in a duly convened meeting perform the functions of the Committee , the decision of the majority of those members shall be the decision of the Committee . (3) The Chairman at any meeting shall have a casting vote in addition to a deliberative vote. (4) No proceedings of the Committee shall be invalid by reason that any member was not present during the whole or part of any proceedings. (5) If the Chairman is not present, the members may elect any member from among their number to chair the meeting . (6) The Secretariat shall convene, at least, four ordinary meetings of the Committee in a calendar year provided that extra-ordinary meetings may be convened when the need arises. (7) In performing the functions entrusted to the Secretariat under these Regulations, the Secretariat shall be deemed to be performing such functions in its ordinary course of business.

provisions.title: Condona­tion
provisions.content: The Committee may, at the request of any party to the proceedings conducted under these Regulations and on good cause shown, condone any non-compliance with the provisions of these Regulations, subject to such conditions as it may, in all the circumstances, thick fit.

provisions.title: Approval of collective agree­ments
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of section 79(2) of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , parties to a collective agreement that provides for minimum services during a strike or lockout may make an application to the Committee in ESC Form No. 8 prescribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations. [Cap. 366] (2) Upon receipt of the application the Committee may require the parties to appear before it and make written or oral representations on their application. (3) Not later than sixty days after the receipt of an application, the Committee shall approve or refuse to approve the relevant provisions of the collective agreement and notify the parties of its decision in writing. (4) The Committee shall provide brief reasons for its refusal to approve the provisions of a collective agreement. (5) Any party aggrieved by the decision of the Committee may appeal to the Labour Court .

provisions.title: Forms
provisions.content: All forms required to be prescribed under these Regulations shall be as prescribed in the First Schedule to these Regulations. First Schedule (Made under regulation 22) [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule (Made under regulation 14) Oath for members of the Committee I,____________________________ having been appointed a Member of the Essential Services Committee, do swear/affirm that I will discharge my functions without bias and will not directly or indirectly reveal any information that comes into my possession without the authorization of the Committee, Commission or Labour Court. SO HELP ME GOD Sworn/Affirmed before me on this________________ day of______ 20______ ________________________ Judge of the High Court History of this document 19 October 2012 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 42 Commenced 03 October 2012 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
